scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with simple HTML returns Document with corresponding p element (normal branch)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); using real HtmlTreeBuilder
pseudocode:
Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); String html = "

test

"; String uri = "http://example.com";

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with empty HTML returns empty body (boundary empty input)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); using real HtmlTreeBuilder
pseudocode: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); String html = ""; String uri = "base";

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with null html throws NullPointerException on StringReader constructor (exception branch)
pseudocode: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); String html = null; String uri = "u";

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) with simple HTML Reader returns Document containing p (normal branch)
scenarios.precondition:
Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Reader reader = new StringReader("

rdr

");
pseudocode: TEST TC04 parseInput Reader simple HTML

Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Reader reader = new StringReader("

rdr

"); String uri = "u";

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) with null Reader throws NullPointerException when lock and parse called (exception branch)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Reader reader = null;
pseudocode: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Reader reader = null; String uri = "u";

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) with Reader throwing IOException causes UncheckedIOException (exception branch)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Reader reader = stub that throws IOException on read (to simulate I/O error)
pseudocode: TEST TC06 parseInput Reader throws IOException
Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Reader reader = new Reader(){public int read(char[] c,int o,int l){throw new IOException("io");}public void close(){} }; String uri="u";

scenarios.description: sequential parseInput(String,String) calls reuse same Parser produce distinct Documents (branch-reentrant)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); using real HtmlTreeBuilder
scenarios.expectedResult: firstDoc != secondDoc; both docs have correct p text
pseudocode: TEST TC07 parseInput sequential calls distinct docs

Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); String html1="

1

"; String html2="

2

"; String uri="u";
Document d1 = parser.parseInput(html1, uri); Document d2 = parser.parseInput(html2, uri);
assert d1 != d2; assert d1.select("p").text().equals("1"); assert d2.select("p").text().equals("2");

scenarios.description: parseInput(String,String) does not change trackPosition setting (branch-state)
pseudocode: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser().setTrackPosition(true); String html=""; String uri="b";

scenarios.description: parseInput(String,String) with custom stub TreeBuilder returns stub Document (branch-custom-builder)
scenarios.precondition: TreeBuilder stubBuilder returns fixed Document dummyDoc from parse; Parser parser = new Parser(stubBuilder);
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document is same dummyDoc instance
pseudocode: TEST TC09 parseInput with stubbed TreeBuilder
Document dummy = new Document(""); TreeBuilder stubBuilder = stub with parse(...) returns dummy; Parser parser = new Parser(stubBuilder); String html="x"; String uri="u";

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader,String) with stub TreeBuilder.parse throwing RuntimeException propagates same exception (exception branch)
scenarios.precondition: TreeBuilder stubBuilder throws IllegalStateException from parse; Parser parser = new Parser(stubBuilder); Reader rdr=new StringReader("x");
pseudocode: TEST TC10 parseInput stub builder throws unchecked
TreeBuilder stubBuilder = stub with parse(...) throws new IllegalStateException("fail"); Parser parser = new Parser(stubBuilder); Reader rdr = new StringReader("x"); String uri="u";
